title: Summarize a Reading
summary: Read a text and write a short summary of the main points in your own words.
learnerPromise: You will be able to identify the main ideas of a text and restate them briefly in your own words.
whyItMatters: Summarizing helps you check that you understood what you read and makes it easier to review later. It is a skill used in most school subjects.
realLifeUses: Writing a short summary of a textbook chapter for study notes
Explaining a news article to someone else in a few sentences
Reviewing research for a project by summarizing each source
Preparing for a book report by capturing the main events or ideas
materialsNeeded: A short article or text to read
result: A 3-5 sentence summary of a text written in your own words
youWillLearn: How to find the main idea of a paragraph or section
How to tell the difference between key points and small details
How to restate ideas using your own words
How to keep a summary short and focused
miniChallenge: Read a short article (1-2 pages) and write a 3-5 sentence summary of the main points in your own words.
steps: Read the full text once without taking notes.
Ask yourself: What is the main topic? What are the most important points?
Write one sentence stating the main idea.
Add 2-4 more sentences covering the key supporting points only.
Check that you did not copy full sentences from the original text.
completionCriteria: Main points are accurate and in your own words
No full sentences were copied from the original
commonProblems: Including too many small details instead of just main points
Copying sentences directly from the text instead of restating
tips: A summary should be much shorter than the original text
If you get stuck, cover the text and explain what you read out loud
Ask: if I told a friend one thing from this, what would it be?
